claim: OBReceiveMFR lacks CE session validation, allowing stale MFRs to be consumed after CE view change

original_gap.finding: Guard is /\ msg.market = m only. No session field on msg is checked. Any msg in msgs_ce_to_ob matching the market is consumable regardless of CE session.
original_gap.snippet:
/\ \E msg \in msgs_ce_to_ob :
    /\ msg.market = m
    /\ LET eff == [type |-> "CEFinalization", ...]

stale_mfrs_persist.finding: CEViewChange UNCHANGED clause lists msgs_ce_to_ob explicitly, confirming that CE view change does not flush or modify the CE-to-OB message channel. MFRs from the old session remain in msgs_ce_to_ob after view change.
stale_mfrs_persist.snippet:
/\ UNCHANGED <<ce_checkpoint_seq,
               msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ce_to_ob, msgs_ob_to_ce, msgs_ob_broadcast,
               obVars, omsVars, cacheVars, archiverVars, obBackupVars>>

fix_session_guard.finding: Modified spec adds /\ msg.session = ob_cached_ce_session[m] as a conjunct in the existential guard. After CE view change updates ob_cached_ce_session[m] to the new session, stale session-1 MFRs no longer satisfy the guard and cannot be consumed.

fix_session_cache_update.finding: Modified spec adds /\ ob_cached_ce_session' = [ob_cached_ce_session EXCEPT ![m] = vc.session] inside the existential block alongside the existing ob_cached_ce_ts update. When OB receives the CE view change message, it atomically advances both its cached timestamp and its cached session. This is what advances ob_cached_ce_session[m] from 1 to 2, after which the session gate in OBReceiveMFR only admits new-session MFRs.

variable_coverage.finding: ob_cached_ce_session is added to cacheVars (line 104 of modified spec). All actions using UNCHANGED <<..., cacheVars, ...>> automatically preserve ob_cached_ce_session. The two actions that individually enumerate cache variables (DownstreamOMSReceiveCEViewChange line 601, DownstreamOMSReceiveOBViewChange line 621) have ob_cached_ce_session added to their UNCHANGED clauses explicitly, ensuring no frame-condition gap.

init_coupling.finding: ob_cached_ce_session = [m \in Markets |-> 1] in Init (line 187 of modified spec) matches ce_primary = 1 (line 164 of original). This coupling is required: without a view change having fired, ob_cached_ce_session[m] = 1 allows initial CE session MFRs through the guard.

attack_trace: 1. CE session=1 produces Fill, sends MFR to msgs_ce_to_ob with session=1 (spec.tla line ~244)
2. CE crashes (CECrash, spec.tla line ~289) before OB consumes the MFR
3. CE view change: new session=2, some effects rolled back (CEViewChange, spec.tla line ~298); msgs_ce_to_ob UNCHANGED, so session=1 MFR persists
4. OBReceiveMFR(m) fires in original spec: msg.market=m matches, no session check, stale session=1 MFR consumed
5. OB sequences phantom CEFinalization referencing a fill CE rolled back
fix_blocks_trace: 4'. After CE VC, OB has not yet processed DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange, so ob_cached_ce_session[m]=1
4''. Once OB processes DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange: ob_cached_ce_session[m] set to 2
4'''. OBReceiveMFR guard: msg.session=1 != ob_cached_ce_session[m]=2 -> predicate false -> action disabled
5'. Stale MFR is blocked; only new session=2 MFRs (produced after CE recovery) can be consumed
